title: Collision Events, Single Resolution, Fragments, Damage, and Lives · Stakeholder Software Mission
summary: A small adaptive-game studio. They need seam-safe wrapping, layered rendering, bounded projectiles, seeded readable asteroids, broad and narrow collision evidence, single response, fragments, lives, and multichannel damage feedback across changed frame rates. Planned artifact: Deliver Toroidal Combat and Collision Slice with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal de…
objectives: Wrap complete object bounds with modular arithmetic and test overshoot across corners and large time steps.
Define clear, background, world, effects, HUD, focus, diagnostics, and presentation order once per frame.
Expire projectiles by world distance or simulation time and remove every group and collision reference exactly once.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Project Toroidal Wrap
steps.instruction:
project case — A project Asteroids team handles case 6191 during a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling. Build deterministic Python evidence to resolve each collision once with explicit destruction, fragments, lives, invulnerability, feedback, and event ordering; keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision; then cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Wrap complete object bounds with modular arithmetic and test overshoot across corners and large time steps.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Project Toroidal Wrap.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for wrap complete object bounds with modular arithmetic and test overshoot across corners and large time steps. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Project Toroidal Wrap, keep Toroidal Wrap connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Single Resolution
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: asteroids-single-resolution-1l39mq" that uses case 1770 to resolve each collision once with explicit destruction, fragments, lives, invulnerability, feedback, and event ordering. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Single Resolution.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Single Resolution into observable performance evidence. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Single Resolution, keep Single Resolution connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-single-resolution-1l39mq
# Competency: asteroids-single-resolution.
# Case 1770: a controller-remapping accessibility review.
# Operating constraint: keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native display, device, audio, file, package, and operating-system effects.
# Changed case: replace an audio-only collision cue with synchronized text, shape, and optional sound evidence.
def evidence_asteroids_single_resolution_1l39mq(collisions: int = 3, resolved_once: int = 3, invulnerability_ms: int = 1200, duplicate_damage: int = 0, fragments_spawned: int = 6) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1l39mq = "1l39mq-qm93l1-1770"
    assert evidence_variant_1l39mq
    evidence_axes_1l39mq = (
        "player1l39mq",
        "seed1770",
        "inputqm93l1",
        "frame1l39mq1770",
        "state17701l39mq",
        "playtestqm93l11770",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1l39mq)) == 6
    if collisions < 0 or resolved_once < 0:
        return False, collisions, "response-domain"
    if resolved_once != collisions:
        return False, resolved_once, "response-count"
    if invulnerability_ms < 0 or duplicate_damage > 0:
        return False, duplicate_damage, "damage-window"
    if fragments_spawned < collisions:
        return False, fragments_spawned, "fragment-policy"
    assert resolved_once == collisions
    return True, fragments_spawned, "single-response"

steps.instruction:
project case — A project Asteroids team handles case 3321 during an offline cross-platform release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to resolve each collision once with explicit destruction, fragments, lives, invulnerability, feedback, and event ordering; retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity; then lose window focus while thrust and fire are held, then prove both actions release safely. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Assign pair identity and terminal ownership so score, deletion, sound, fragments, and telemetry happen once.
